content: I'm trying to recreate that amazing Thai green curry I had in Bangkok last year. The flavors just aren't quite right.
Thai curries can be tricky! What ingredients are you using in your curry paste?
I've been using store-bought paste plus some fresh lemongrass and kaffir lime leaves. I'm Finley, by the way. I'm an opera singer and just got back from a tour in Vienna.
Nice to meet you, Finley! Making your own curry paste from scratch makes a huge difference. Do you have a mortar and pestle?
I do! I actually picked one up in Chiang Mai during that trip. Willow keeps trying to play with it though - she's my new golden retriever rescue.
Aw, Willow sounds adorable! A mortar and pestle is perfect for curry paste. The key is really pounding the ingredients to release their oils.
I'll give that a try this weekend. I've been so busy with rehearsals for La Bohème at the opera house. We open in three weeks!
That's exciting! Break a leg with the rehearsals. For your curry, don't forget to balance the flavors - sweet, sour, salty, and spicy.
I've been struggling with that balance. I tend to make it too spicy. I'm more comfortable with Italian flavors from my time studying in Milan.
Thai cuisine is all about that delicate balance. Start with less chili and build up. What protein are you using?
I've been using chicken thighs. They stay so tender. I also love adding bamboo shoots and Thai eggplant from the Asian market near my apartment.
Great choices! Chicken thighs are perfect. Have you tried making the curry with homemade coconut milk?
Not yet, but I'm planning to. I've been getting into gardening too - growing Thai basil and lemongrass on my balcony. It's so satisfying to cook with ingredients you've grown yourself.
That's wonderful! Fresh herbs make such a difference. Your balcony garden sounds perfect for Thai cooking. Would you like my recipe for authentic green curry paste?
